product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Luxembourg MiFID II training concept that tries to package a CSSF-recognised external route with multilingual delivery and compliance-oriented certification.
product_summary.target_user: Financial institutions and staff under CSSF prudential supervision who provide investment information or advice.
product_summary.core_pain: Regulated financial firms need a credible external training path that maps to MiFID II knowledge-and-competence expectations and survives compliance scrutiny.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: A CSSF-recognised training path, completion certificate and audit documentation.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The product is driven by regulated training, certification and CSSF-recognition language.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: The actual customer is still a regulated financial firm buying training for staff.
A partner or accredited provider route may be needed for credibility.

budget_owner.reason: Compliance or risk functions are the most likely budget owners for this type of training.

buyer_influencers.reason: Legal is likely to review wording around accreditation, acceptance and regulated-practice claims.
Finance leadership may care about operational burden, multilingual delivery and partner economics.
Compliance teams will decide whether the route is defensible.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Circular CSSF 17/670 sets criteria for external training on knowledge and competence in the MiFID II framework.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: CSSF’s circular explicitly sets minimum criteria for external training in the MiFID II / knowledge-and-competence framework.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: CSSF maintains a list of bodies providing external training within the meaning of Circular 17/670.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The CSSF publishes the list and the public MiFID II / PRIIPs page points to it.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: House of Training has a CSSF-accredited MiFID II certification path.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: House of Training publicly states that its MiFID II certification programme has received CSSF accreditation.

Public House of Training materials show a 64-hour one-stop route without optional e-learning or 76 hours with optional e-learning, and the current certification page uses a modular certification path. The card’s universal 60h claim is materially misleading.

Guichet says co-funding does not apply to mandatory continuing training legally required to practise a regulated profession, while House of Training says its MiFID II training can be eligible for the co-financing law. The product-level claim is therefore conflicted and should not be treated as settled.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: House of Training is FR/EN only.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The reviewed House of Training MiFID II certification page offers English and French delivery.
The public sources reviewed do not establish an exhaustive market census across Luxembourg or neighbouring markets.
No official CSSF deadline matching this claim was found in the reviewed sources.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: All financial sector employees providing investment services (73K in Luxembourg).
externally_checkable_claims.notes: No primary source reviewed here confirmed the 73K count.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: This proposed product can issue a CSSF-accredited completion certificate.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed sources confirm an incumbent accredited route, not accreditation for this new product.
No public source reviewed confirms this deadline.
The claim would require an exhaustive market review that was not established by the sources reviewed.
No primary source confirmed the exact figure.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The product can be delivered as a new independent certified route without partner/accreditation work.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The public evidence points the opposite way: a formal route is already controlled by the CSSF-listed accredited ecosystem.

regulator_entities.name: Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF)
regulator_entities.relevance: Publishes Circular 17/670 and maintains the list of bodies providing external training.

regulator_entities.name: European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA)
regulator_entities.relevance: Issued the knowledge-and-competence guidelines that underpin the CSSF circular.

framework_entities.name: Circular CSSF 17/670
framework_entities.relevance: Defines external training criteria for MiFID II knowledge and competence.

framework_entities.name: MiFID II / Directive 2014/65/EU
framework_entities.relevance: The broader EU investment-services framework that underlies the training regime.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC / Guichet.lu
funding_bodies.relevance: Relevant because the card claims company training co-funding and the public rules are conflicted for regulated training.

approval_entities.name: CSSF list of bodies providing external training within the meaning of Circular 17/670
approval_entities.relevance: This is the public route an external provider must understand before making accreditation-style claims.

approval_entities.name: House of Training MiFID II Certification route
approval_entities.relevance: Public incumbent route that shows what a recognised path currently looks like.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Knowledge-and-competence criteria apply to staff providing investment information or advice under MiFID II.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Investment-information and investment-advice staff under CSSF prudential supervision

regulatory_requirements.requirement: External training bodies can be registered/listed by CSSF for the Circular 17/670 route.

subsidies_or_funding.name: Luxembourg company training co-funding
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: Guichet excludes mandatory continuing training for regulated professions, while House of Training publicly says its MiFID II route can be eligible for the co-financing law. The conflict remains unresolved for this new product.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - MiFID II Certification
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: CSSF-accredited incumbent route with module-based training and exam path.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - MiFID II - Framework Overview
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public MiFID II overview course in English and French.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - MiFID II - Risk, Compliance, Regulations
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Another public Luxembourg route covering compliance and regulation modules.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The product uses accredited, recognised and compliance-sensitive wording in a regulated finance context.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: A credible delivery in the intended form needs a formal route, listing or accredited partner ecosystem.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about CSSF accreditation or acceptance depend on formal route control, not just content quality.

proof_stack.reason: The product needs a recognised route before the strongest claims can be used.
Compliance buyers will care whether the training trail can survive scrutiny.
The market must still buy the multilingual proposition even if the route exists.

proof_burden.reason: This product depends on regulated acceptance and formal route credibility, which makes proof burden very high.

dependency_types.reason: A formal accredited or listed route is central to the value proposition.
The strongest claims depend on how CSSF treats the route.
A credible external finance expert or partner is effectively necessary.
The subsidy angle is part of the positioning and must be validated carefully.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current scope is very specific: Luxembourg MiFID II training for in-scope financial staff.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Other countries will use similar frameworks but different approval structures and provider routes.

subsidy_exists.reason: Company training co-funding exists in Luxembourg.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: Official guidance and incumbent provider wording conflict on whether this category of training is co-financing eligible.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: The public subsidy story is not clean and should not be presented as settled.

market_regulatory_context.reason: This sits directly inside regulated financial-services knowledge and competence rules.

claim_language_risk.reason: The card uses accreditation, recognition and mandatory language that can easily outrun the public evidence.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: A formal CSSF route is central to the product's credibility.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: A CSSF-accredited incumbent route already exists, so multilingual differentiation alone is insufficient.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The 60h mandate and 73K audience claims are not supported as stated.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: Official and provider wording conflict on training-aid eligibility for this category.

positioning_risk.reason: The product may be a new business line requiring route-orchestration rather than a simple training offer.

claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The public materials show a modular accredited route with 64h or 76h in one House of Training description and a separate certification path on the current page. The card's universal 60h wording is misleading.

Guichet excludes mandatory regulated-profession training, while House of Training says its MiFID II route can be eligible. The public wording conflicts and does not support a clean claim for this new product.
The reviewed sources confirm an incumbent accredited route, not accreditation for this proposed product.
No official CSSF deadline matching this statement was found; an exam date is not the same as a regulatory deadline.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Decide whether the business will pursue its own CSSF route, use a partner route or remain a non-accredited training reseller.
Remove any subsidy copy until eligibility is resolved at product level.
Re-test whether language coverage is truly a differentiator after accounting for the incumbent route.
